commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Tiguan Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Early 1.4 TSI & 2.0 TDI Tiguan Engines Fail
problems.rootCause: Early tensioner design loses hydraulic pressure on cold starts, allowing chain slack and potential catastrophic jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, chain, guides before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; not suitable once rattling starts

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; 12-month warranty; addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: If you hear chain rattle on cold start, stop driving immediately - continued use risks valve/piston damage. For preventative maintenance under 60k miles, a specialist chain kit replacement offers good value. Once symptoms appear or mileage exceeds 80k, a rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner is the safer long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CAV/CTH/BKD engine prices for your Volkswagen Tiguan

problems.h4: DPF & EGR Clogging - Why Urban Tiguan 2.0 TDI Owners Face Repeated Issues
problems.rootCause: Short urban journeys prevent DPF regeneration; EGR cooler carbonisation restricts flow and triggers limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car DPF cleaning, EGR valve/cooler decarbonising
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; issues likely to recur on same driving pattern

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with cleaned/replaced DPF & EGR components

problems.recommendation: If your Tiguan does mostly short trips, DPF/EGR issues are almost inevitable. A professional clean can buy 12-18 months, but for high-mileage vehicles or repeated failures, a rebuilt engine with addressed emissions components offers better long-term value and reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CFFB/CRLB Volkswagen Tiguan diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves - Why 2.0 TSI Tiguan Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design means fuel doesn't wash intake valves; oil vapour from PCV creates hard carbon deposits.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast valves with walnut media to remove carbon
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for 20-30k miles; deposits will return without PCV modification

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with walnut-blasted heads and updated PCV

problems.recommendation: If you're experiencing misfires, rough idle or reduced power on a 2.0 TSI Tiguan, carbon buildup is the likely culprit. Walnut blasting is cost-effective for vehicles under 70k miles. For higher mileage or repeated issues, a rebuilt engine with addressed PCV and cleaned components offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CHHB/CULC Volkswagen Tiguan petrol engine prices

problems.h4: Injector Failure - Why High-Mileage Tiguan 2.0 TDI Engines Develop Misfires
problems.rootCause: Piezo or solenoid injectors wear internally; poor fuel quality accelerates tip erosion and seal failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only one injector failed; others may follow soon

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with calibrated injectors, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; all injectors renewed; 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If one injector has failed on a high-mileage 2.0 TDI Tiguan, the others are likely close behind. Replacing a single injector can be economical short-term, but for vehicles over 100k miles or with multiple fault codes, a rebuilt engine with all-new calibrated injectors offers better value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BKD/DFG Volkswagen Tiguan diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: High Oil Consumption - Why Early 1.5 TSI Tiguan Engines Need Frequent Top-Ups
problems.rootCause: Early PCV system design allows excess oil vapour into intake; piston ring design contributes to consumption.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - PCV Valve + Catch Can Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, fit oil catch can to reduce vapour ingress
repairOptions.longevity: Reduces consumption but doesn't eliminate; monitoring still required

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated PCV and piston rings
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; addresses root cause; 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your DADA-engine Tiguan is using over 1L oil per 1,000 miles, the early PCV design is likely at fault. A catch can upgrade can help, but for severe consumption or vehicles under warranty claim thresholds, a rebuilt engine with updated components offers the only permanent fix.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DADA Volkswagen Tiguan 1.5 TSI engine prices

problems.h4: P0011 Fault Code - Why Tiguan Engines Show Camshaft Timing Errors
problems.rootCause: Timing chain stretch or variable valve timing solenoid faults cause cam/crank correlation errors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace camshaft position solenoids, fresh oil/filter, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if chain is within tolerance; diagnostic essential first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with new timing components, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution for stretched chain; 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: P0011 can indicate anything from dirty oil to a stretched chain. Start with a professional diagnostic. If chain stretch is confirmed (via tensioner position or noise), replacement is the only safe option - continued driving risks catastrophic interference damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for P0011 Volkswagen Tiguan engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Tiguan
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with timing chain rattle, DPF warnings, carbon-related misfires or injector faults, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volkswagen Tiguan specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Tiguan Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Tiguan and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Volkswagenmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Volkswagen Tiguan Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Volkswagen Tiguan engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Volkswagen Tiguan Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally inspected, cleaned and tested units with worn components replaced; ideal for budget-conscious Tiguan owners.
types.frontDescription: Professionally inspected, cleaned and tested units with worn components replaced; ideal for budget-conscious Tiguan owners.
types.frontDisclaimer: Availability varies by engine code and trim; confirm fitment before ordering.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Tiguan engines undergo multi-point inspection, carbon cleaning and replacement of seals/gaskets. Common codes like BKD, CFFB, CHHB are regularly available. Ideal for vehicles under 100k miles with no major internal damage.
backBullets: Best value for early first-gen models
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Volkswagen Tiguan engine prices

types.title: Volkswagen Tiguan Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Fully disassembled, machined and rebuilt with upgraded components addressing known Tiguan failure points.
types.frontDescription: Fully disassembled, machined and rebuilt with upgraded components addressing known Tiguan failure points.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price depends on engine code; performance variants cost more.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Tiguan engines feature new pistons, bearings, seals and upgraded timing components. Addresses CAV/CTH tensioner issues, CHHB carbon buildup and CRLB EGR problems. Bench-tested before dispatch.
backBullets: Suitable for all generations and variants
types.cta: Get rebuilt Volkswagen Tiguan engine quotes

types.title: Volkswagen Tiguan Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage donor units from accident-damaged vehicles; budget option for out-of-warranty Tiguan models.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage donor units from accident-damaged vehicles; budget option for out-of-warranty Tiguan models.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage and condition vary; compression test recommended before fitting.
types.backDescription: Used Tiguan engines offer the lowest upfront cost. Verify compression, service history and absence of timing chain rattle or DPF warnings. Best for vehicles with known service records and under 80k miles.
backBullets: Significant cost savings vs rebuilt options
types.cta: Check used Volkswagen Tiguan engine availability

types.title: Volkswagen Tiguan Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Factory-spec restoration to original tolerances using OEM or premium aftermarket components throughout.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec restoration to original tolerances using OEM or premium aftermarket components throughout.
types.frontDisclaimer: Lead times may be longer; confirm exact specification before ordering.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured Tiguan engines are restored to factory specifications with precision machining and OEM-grade components. Ideal for late-model SEL, R-Line and Allspace variants where longevity is prioritised.
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Volkswagen Tiguan engine costs

types.title: Volkswagen Tiguan Refurbished Engines
types.description: Targeted repair of specific faults (e.g. timing chain, injectors) without full rebuild; cost-effective for known issues.
types.frontDescription: Targeted repair of specific faults (e.g. timing chain, injectors) without full rebuild; cost-effective for known issues.
types.frontDisclaimer: Only suitable for engines with isolated, diagnosable faults; full inspection required.
types.backDescription: Refurbished Tiguan engines address specific failures like CAV tensioner replacement, CFF injector renewal or CHHB walnut blasting. Cost-effective when damage is contained and block/head are sound.
backBullets: Focused repair of known failure points
Best for vehicles with single-issue diagnostics
types.cta: View refurbished Volkswagen Tiguan engine options

types.title: Volkswagen Tiguan Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Complete engine replacement service including delivery, fitting, fluids and post-installation testing nationwide.
types.frontDescription: Complete engine replacement service including delivery, fitting, fluids and post-installation testing nationwide.
types.frontDisclaimer: Fitment cost varies by workshop location and engine complexity; 4Motion/DSG models cost more.
types.backDescription: Supply & fit service includes engine delivery, professional installation by Tiguan-specialist garages, fluid refresh, ECU coding and road test. Essential for 4Motion AWD and DSG-equipped models requiring precise calibration.
backBullets: Ideal for daily drivers needing minimal downtime

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen Tiguan Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Volkswagen Tiguan 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L TDI is the backbone of the Tiguan diesel range across both generations (2007-present), powering S, SE, SEL, R-Line and Allspace variants. EA188 and EA288 architecture delivers strong torque and motorway economy, though urban driving patterns can accelerate DPF and EGR issues.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Tiguan 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Tiguan 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L TSI powered entry-level first-generation Tiguan models (2007-2016) with twin-charging for strong low-end response. CAV/CTH codes are popular but require vigilance on timing chain tensioner condition, especially on cold starts.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Tiguan 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Tiguan 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced in 2017 for second-generation models, the 1.5L TSI (EA211 evo) features cylinder deactivation (ACT) for improved efficiency. Early DADA units had PCV-related oil consumption; later DPC/DXD codes feature updated systems.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Tiguan 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Tiguan 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L TSI (EA888 Gen 3/3B) powers performance-oriented SEL, R-Line and Black Edition variants from 2016-present. Strong power delivery but direct injection design requires attention to intake valve carbon deposits.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Tiguan 2.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Volkswagen Tiguan Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Volkswagen Tiguan 1.4L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The GTE plug-in hybrid combines a 1.4L TSI (CZDA) with an electric motor for 245hp combined output and ~30 miles electric-only range. Specialist knowledge required for hybrid system diagnostics during engine replacement. Not sure which engine size your Volkswagen Tiguan needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a Volkswagenmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Volkswagen Tiguan specialists.
commonFailurePoints: coolant pump failures affecting thermal management
carbon buildup on direct injection valves

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Tiguan Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Volkswagen Tiguan's production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: First Generation Launch: EA188 Diesel & EA111 Petrol
years.description: The original Tiguan launched with robust EA188 2.0 TDI diesels and twin-charged EA111 1.4 TSI petrols, establishing the model's UK popularity.
keyChanges: Volkswagen Tiguan enters UK market with Typ 5N platform
2.0 TDI BKD/AZV engines dominate early sales (70% of registrations)
1.4 TSI CAV twin-charged petrol introduced for lower-tax bands
4Motion AWD and DSG automatic options available from launch
knownFor: Strong early sales establishing Tiguan as UK SUV favourite
BKD/AZV timing chain tensioner wear emerges as key concern
CAV 1.4 TSI cold-start rattle reported by early adopters
Robust build quality but complex electronics for the era
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2010 Volkswagen Tiguan replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Tiguan 2007 engine replacement • BKD timing chain tensioner failure • CAV cold start rattle fix

years.preview: First Generation Facelift: Updated EA188 & EA111
years.description: Mid-life update introduced revised engine codes with improved emissions and minor reliability refinements, though core failure patterns persisted.
keyChanges: Facelift styling and interior updates for Typ 5N
CFFB/CFGB diesel codes replace early BKD/AZV in many trims
CTH 1.4 TSI introduced with revised timing components
Euro 5 emissions compliance across the range
mainEngines: CTH - 1.4 petrol revised tensioner (SE / SEL / Match)
knownFor: CFF/CFGB DPF clogging becomes common on urban-use vehicles
CTH timing chain still requires monitoring despite revisions
Strong residual values support engine replacement over vehicle change
EGR valve carbonisation emerges as frequent service item
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2015 Volkswagen Tiguan replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Tiguan 2013 engine replacement • CFFB DPF blocked warning • CTH timing chain P0011 code

years.preview: Second Generation Launch: EA288 Diesel & EA888/EA211 Petrol
years.description: All-new Typ AD/BW platform introduced modern EA288 diesel and EA888/EA211 petrol engines with improved efficiency but new failure patterns.
keyChanges: All-new MQB-based Typ AD/BW platform replaces Typ 5N
EA288 CRLB diesel replaces EA188; EA888 CHHB petrol introduced
1.5 TSI DADA replaces 1.4 TSI for second-gen entry petrol
Allspace 7-seat variant launched with shared engine codes
mainEngines: CRBC / CRLB - 2.0 diesel EA288 (SE / SEL / R-Line)
knownFor: CRLB EGR cooler cracking becomes recall item on second-gen diesels
CHHB carbon buildup on intake valves affects performance and idle
DADA early PCV design leads to oil consumption complaints
Allspace shares standard Tiguan engines but with LWB-specific calibration
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2019 Volkswagen Tiguan replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Tiguan 2017 engine replacement • CRLB EGR cooler recall • CHHB carbon buildup walnut blasting

years.preview: Second Generation Update: EA288 Evo & GTE PHEV
years.description: Late second-gen models received updated EA288 evo diesel engines and the GTE plug-in hybrid variant, adding electrification to the range.
keyChanges: DTE/DTUA EA288 evo diesel with mild-hybrid assistance introduced
CZDA 1.4 TSI PHEV powers new GTE plug-in hybrid variant
DKFA replaces CHHB for late-model 2.0 TSI applications
Enhanced infotainment and driver assistance across the range
mainEngines: DFG / DTE - 2.0 diesel EA288 evo (SEL / R-Line / Allspace)
knownFor: DTE mild-hybrid system adds complexity but improves urban efficiency
GTE PHEV requires specialist knowledge for hybrid system diagnostics
DKFA improves thermal management over earlier CHHB units
Strong demand for rebuilt engines on high-mileage fleet and lease returns
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2023 Volkswagen Tiguan replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Tiguan 2021 engine replacement • GTE hybrid system fault • DTE AdBlue dosing pump failure

years.preview: Present - Third Generation Launch: Latest EA211/EA888 Evo
years.description: The all-new third-generation Tiguan features the latest EA211 evo and EA888 evo engines with enhanced efficiency, emissions compliance and connectivity.
keyChanges: All-new Typ BW platform with updated styling and technology
DXD 1.5 TSI and latest EA888 evo 2.0 TSI for petrol variants
DTE/DTUA EA288 evo continues for diesel applications
Enhanced electrification with 48V mild-hybrid across many variants
mainEngines: DXD - 1.5 petrol EA211 evo (SE / SEL / R-Line)
Latest EA888 evo - 2.0 petrol (R-Line / Black Edition)
DTE / DTUA - 2.0 diesel EA288 evo (SE / SEL / Allspace)
knownFor: Early reliability data still emerging; monitor carbon buildup and turbo reliability
48V mild-hybrid systems add service complexity but improve efficiency
Strong demand for rebuilt engines on outgoing second-gen models continues
Third-gen pricing supports engine replacement over new vehicle purchase for many owners
years.cta: Get quotes for 2024 Volkswagen Tiguan replacement engines

faq.h2: Volkswagen Tiguan Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volkswagen Tiguan engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Volkswagen Tiguan engine typically costs £850-£2,800 supply-only depending on engine code and variant, with 2.0 TDI units at the lower end and performance 2.0 TSI or GTE PHEV at the higher end. Supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on workshop location.
keyPoints: 2.0 TDI rebuilt engines: £850-£2,400 supply only
2.0 TSI rebuilt engines: £1,350-£2,800 supply only
1.4/1.5 TSI rebuilt engines: £950-£2,300 supply only
Supply & fit service adds £450-£850 to engine price
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Tiguan engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most reliable engine for the Volkswagen Tiguan?
items.answer: The later EA288 2.0 TDI (CRLB/DFG) and EA888 Gen 3 2.0 TSI (CHHB/DKFA) are generally considered the most reliable Volkswagen Tiguan engines when properly maintained, with fewer systemic issues than early EA188 diesels or EA111 1.4 TSI petrols.
keyPoints: CRLB/DFG 2.0 TDI: Improved EGR design, fewer timing concerns
CHHB/DKFA 2.0 TSI: Better thermal management, reduced carbon issues
Regular servicing and quality oil critical for longevity
Avoid early CAV 1.4 TSI without verified tensioner upgrade
items.cta: Find rebuilt CRLB/CHHB Volkswagen Tiguan engines with warranty

items.question: Is the Volkswagen Tiguan 1.4 TSI timing chain problem fixed?
items.answer: Later CTH 1.4 TSI units received revised tensioners, but the fundamental design risk remains. For CAV engines, only a rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner components offers a definitive fix for the cold-start rattle and potential catastrophic failure.
keyPoints: CAV engines: High failure risk; upgraded tensioner essential
CTH engines: Improved but still monitor for rattle on cold start
P0011 fault code indicates chain stretch requiring attention
Rebuilt engines include upgraded timing components as standard
items.cta: Get quotes for CAV/CTH Volkswagen Tiguan engine replacement

items.question: Will a Volkswagen Golf engine fit my Tiguan?
items.answer: Many Volkswagen Tiguan engines share architecture with contemporary Golf models (MQB platform), but fitment depends on exact engine code, mounting points, and 4Motion/DSG compatibility - always verify via registration lookup before ordering.
keyPoints: Same engine code doesn't guarantee direct fitment
4Motion AWD and DSG models have specific calibration requirements
ECU coding may be required after engine swap
Use DVLA registration check to confirm exact compatibility
items.cta: Verify your Volkswagen Tiguan engine compatibility instantly

items.question: Are Volkswagen Tiguan diesel engines ULEZ compliant?
items.answer: Volkswagen Tiguan 2.0 TDI engines meeting Euro 6 emissions (typically CRBC/CRLB/DFG codes from late 2015 onwards) are ULEZ compliant in London; earlier Euro 5 BKD/CFF units are not exempt and incur the daily charge.
keyPoints: Euro 6 diesel required for London ULEZ exemption
Check V5C logbook for emissions standard confirmation
DPF-equipped models more likely to meet Euro 6
Non-compliant diesels face £12.50/day London ULEZ charge
items.cta: Compare ULEZ-compliant Volkswagen Tiguan diesel engine prices

items.question: How long should a Volkswagen Tiguan engine last?
items.answer: With proper servicing, Volkswagen Tiguan engines can exceed 150,000 miles, though common failure points like timing chains (CAV), DPFs (urban TDI), and carbon buildup (TSI) often require attention between 60k-100k miles to reach that milestone.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with correct VW specification critical
Address warning lights promptly to prevent cascade damage
Urban driving accelerates DPF/EGR issues on diesel models
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can extend life significantly
items.cta: Find long-life rebuilt Volkswagen Tiguan engines with warranty

items.question: What causes a Volkswagen Tiguan to go into limp mode?
items.answer: Limp mode on Volkswagen Tiguan models is commonly triggered by DPF regeneration failure, EGR valve faults, turbo boost leaks, or P0011 timing correlation errors - diagnostic scanning is essential to identify the specific cause before deciding on repair versus replacement.
keyPoints: DPF warning light often precedes limp mode on diesel models
EGR valve carbonisation restricts flow triggering protection mode
Turbo boost leaks cause under-boost faults and power loss
P0011 code indicates timing issues requiring immediate attention
items.cta: Diagnose your Volkswagen Tiguan limp mode cause with a specialist

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Volkswagen Tiguan engine or buying another car?
items.answer: For Volkswagen Tiguan models in good overall condition with service history, engine replacement (£850-£2,800) is typically far more economical than purchasing a comparable used vehicle (£8,000-£15,000+), especially when backed by a 12-month warranty.
keyPoints: Compare replacement cost against current vehicle value
Factor in remaining life of transmission, suspension, interior
Rebuilt engines with warranty offer predictable long-term cost
Avoid replacement only if chassis/corrosion issues exist
items.cta: Calculate if Volkswagen Tiguan engine replacement is right for you

items.question: Where can I find the engine code on my Volkswagen Tiguan?
items.answer: Your Volkswagen Tiguan engine code is stamped on the engine block near the oil filter housing, and also appears on the vehicle data sticker (usually in the service book, under the spare wheel cover, or on the driver's door shut).
keyPoints: Engine code format: 3-4 letters (e.g., BKD, CRLB, CHHB)
Data sticker also shows PR codes for exact specification
Registration lookup instantly confirms engine code and variant
Always quote engine code when requesting replacement quotes
items.cta: Identify your Volkswagen Tiguan engine code in seconds

items.question: Do Volkswagen Tiguan engines need special oil?
items.answer: Yes - all Volkswagen Tiguan engines require oil meeting VW specification 504.00/507.00 (petrol/diesel) or 508.00/509.00 (latest models); using incorrect oil can accelerate timing chain wear, DPF clogging, or turbo failure.
keyPoints: 5W-30 synthetic meeting VW 507.00 standard for most models
Latest EA211/EA888 evo may require 0W-20 VW 508.00
Check owner's manual or V5C for exact specification
Incorrect oil voids warranty and accelerates component wear
items.cta: Source correct-spec oil with your Volkswagen Tiguan engine replacement
